title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
summary: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a framework for building agents that integrate with data sources like BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB using standardized connectors. The ADK reduces the need for custom database connector development by implementing Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool connections.
writeup: This video covers building agents with Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit (ADK) and connecting them to data sources including BigQuery, Google Maps, and AlloyDB through standardized connectors. The ADK implements the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tool connections, which cuts the need to write custom database connectors. The session also addresses agent orchestration and observability as part of AgentOps.
